title: Getting Started with Vue.js
content:
### Vue CLI / create-vue

Vue 3 recommends using `create-vue` — the official project scaffolding tool built on Vite.

### Creating a New Project

```bash
npm create vue@latest
```

You'll be prompted to select optional features like TypeScript, Vue Router, Pinia, and testing tools.

### Project Structure

```
my-vue-app/
├── index.html          # Entry HTML file
├── vite.config.js      # Vite configuration
├── package.json
├── public/
└── src/
    ├── main.js         # App entry point
    ├── App.vue         # Root component
    ├── components/     # Reusable components
    ├── assets/         # Static assets
    └── router/         # Router config (if installed)
```

### The Entry Point (main.js)

```js
import { createApp } from 'vue'
import App from './App.vue'
import router from './router'

const app = createApp(App)
app.use(router)
app.mount('#app')
```

### Running the Dev Server

```bash
npm run dev
```

This starts Vite's dev server with hot module replacement (HMR). Changes to your components reflect instantly.

### createApp and Mounting

- `createApp(component)` creates the application instance
- `app.mount(selector)` mounts the root component to the DOM
- `app.use(plugin)` installs plugins like Vue Router or Pinia

quiz.question: Which command scaffolds a new Vue 3 project?
What function is used to create a Vue application instance?
